name: Castillo de Malpica – circular from Talavera de la Reina
name_original: Castillo de Malpica – circular desde Talavera de la Reina
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Talavera De La Reina are set within the province of Toledo, Spain, characterized by its riverine environment at the confluence of the Tagus and Alberche rivers. The region features fertile plains and extends into the "Toledo steppe," offering diverse terrain for cyclists. Many routes utilize well-paved surfaces and incorporate historical bridges over the Tagus, providing scenic views and varied challenges. Distant views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains can also be observed from some areas. Best road cycling routes around Talavera De La Reina The most popular road cycling route is Calera y Chozas - Velada – circular from Talavera de la Reina by the Puente de Castilla-La Mancha, a 29.3 miles (47.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the Puente de Castilla-La Mancha. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de San Vicente – circular from Talavera de la Reina to Illán de Vacas, a moderate 45.6 miles (73.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain as it heads towards the Sierra de San Vicente. Local road cyclists also love the El Piélago Pass – Talavera de la Reina loop from Talavera de la Reina, a 42.8 miles (68.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with substantial climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 29 minutes. Road cycling around Talavera De La Reina is defined by its riverine plains, historical bridges, and the open landscapes of the Toledo steppe. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Talavera De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 6 reviews. More than 1,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Talavera De La Reina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Talavera de la Reina, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Talavera de la Reina. These include 13 easy routes, 23 moderate routes, and 21 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Talavera de la Reina?
A: Road cycling routes in Talavera de la Reina offer a diverse landscape. You'll find routes along the fertile plains of the Tagus and Alberche rivers, extending into the open landscapes of the "Toledo steppe." While many routes feature well-paved surfaces, some, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20138653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Piélago Pass – Talavera de la Reina loop&lt;/a&gt;, include substantial climbs and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, Talavera de la Reina offers numerous circular routes. For an easy ride with scenic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023011345/calera-y-chozas-velada-circular-from-talavera-de-la-reina-by-the-puente-de-castilla-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calera y Chozas - Velada circular&lt;/a&gt;. For a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022545920/sierra-de-san-vicente-circular-from-talavera-de-la-reina-to-illan-de-vacas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Vicente circular&lt;/a&gt; offers significant elevation gain. There are options for all abilities.

Q: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes incorporate historical bridges over the Tagus River, such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4966496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilla-La Mancha Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique views. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5190803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reina Sofía Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. From the outskirts of the city, distant views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains add to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' road cycling routes are not explicitly detailed for young children, the region's commitment to ecotourism and the presence of easy, mostly paved routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023011345/calera-y-chozas-velada-circular-from-talavera-de-la-reina-by-the-puente-de-castilla-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calera y Chozas - Velada circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 47.2 km with minimal elevation, could be suitable for families with older, experienced children. The Tagus Riverbank Trail also offers a tranquil, less challenging environment.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Talavera de la Reina?
A: Talavera de la Reina experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass by historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to showcase the region's heritage. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029363574/castillo-de-malpica-circular-from-talavera-de-la-reina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Malpica circular route&lt;/a&gt; takes you towards the historic Malpica Castle. Within Talavera de la Reina itself, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5191721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Francisco&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5191144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salvador Meeting and Culture Center&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of urban or peri-urban cycling itineraries.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Talavera de la Reina?
A: The road cycling routes around Talavera de la Reina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from riverine plains to the Toledo steppe, and the inclusion of iconic landmarks like the historical bridges over the Tagus. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there options for advanced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. For experienced road cyclists looking for a significant challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina – Main Square of Pelahustán loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 84.7 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20138653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Piélago Pass – Talavera de la Reina loop&lt;/a&gt;, with similar elevation, offer demanding climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: What are the typical road surfaces like on the routes?
A: Many cycling routes in and around Talavera de la Reina feature mostly paved surfaces, which is ideal for road cycling. The region's infrastructure supports a smooth riding experience, though as with any area, conditions can vary, so always be prepared for minor surface changes.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with other outdoor activities in the region?
A: Yes, the region around Talavera de la Reina is rich in outdoor opportunities. Beyond cycling, you can explore hiking trails, urban walks highlighting ceramic murals, and peri-urban routes along the Tagus. For those willing to venture further, Cabañeros National Park offers extensive hiking and nature observation opportunities.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer views of the Tagus River?
A: Many routes in the area leverage the city's location on the Tagus River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023011345/calera-y-chozas-velada-circular-from-talavera-de-la-reina-by-the-puente-de-castilla-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calera y Chozas - Velada circular&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers views of the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4966496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilla-La Mancha Bridge&lt;/a&gt; over the Tagus, and other routes often follow the riverbanks, providing picturesque riverside landscapes.
tips: Its 152 cables and 192 meters make it the second highest cable-stayed bridge in Spain and the second in Europe. It is a display of national and international architecture.

This castle, privately owned by the Duchy of Arion, is not open to the public and cannot be visited inside, although it is probably worth it since it has the peculiarity of having been continuously inhabited for centuries. The most we can do is surround it or observe it from the opposite bank of the river, which gives it an unusual atmosphere in the medieval architecture of the Iberian Peninsula.

It extends along the eastern bank of the Tagus River and is the second most populated municipality in the province and the fourth in Castilla-La Mancha.
Among its monuments are the Basilica of Our Lady of the Prado, which houses what is today the patron saint of the city. It was called Queen of the Hermitages by Philip II, it is also called the Sistine Chapel of ceramics, because this colorful art shows here all its splendor from the 16th century to the present day. The style of its construction is a combination of Renaissance and Baroque, although it has undergone various restorations over the centuries. It was elevated to the dignity of Basilica in 1989 by Pope John Paul II.
In the Church of Santa María La Mayor (La Colegial) you can admire its pure Gothic cloister, which was built around the year 1469. Its floor plan is rectangular, made up of three longitudinal naves and without a transept. In the years 1704 and 1705 the two upper sections of the Tower were added.
